title: Cladothrix lanuginosa
taxonomy: Cladothrix lanuginosa is classified as a species of bacteria within the phylum Pseudomonadota and the family Rhodospirillaceae. It belongs to the genus Cladothrix. This bacterium is commonly found in aquatic environments and has been isolated from various water sources. Cladothrix lanuginosa is a Gram-negative, rod-shaped bacterium that is capable of photosynthesis. It is often studied for its ability to produce pigments and other compounds of interest to biotechnology and medicine.
